ticketTitle: Add an unsupported-units banner to the report card: surface the count and language list, and exclude unsupported units from aggregate grade calculations.

nonGoals: Do not modify GenerateCard or any formatters
Do not change aggregates
Do not add banner text
Do not modify badge.go, health.go, detailed.go, full.go, report_tree.go
Do not create testdata fixtures
deliverables.planText:
1. Read internal/analysis/ to confirm ForLanguage signature and nil-means-unsupported contract.
2. In internal/report/card.go, add three fields to the Card struct: UnsupportedCount int, UnsupportedLanguages []string, AnalyzableUnits int.
3. Add a package-private helper func partitionByLanguage(records []CertificationRecord) (supported, unsupported []CertificationRecord) that calls analysis.ForLanguage(unit.Language) and partitions accordingly.
4. Run go test ./... -count=1 — all existing tests must pass (new fields default to zero values, no caller yet).
deliverables.objective: Extend the Card struct in card.go with UnsupportedCount, UnsupportedLanguages fields, and add a private partitionByLanguage helper that splits []CertificationRecord into supported/unsupported slices using analysis.ForLanguage. Zero callers — no behavior change.

nonGoals: Do not add banner text to FormatCardText or FormatCardMarkdown
Do not modify badge.go, health.go, detailed.go, full.go, report_tree.go
Do not create new testdata or new test cases
Do not change the Card struct fields (already added in deliverable 0)
deliverables.planText:
1. In internal/report/card.go, inside GenerateCard (or its callees), call partitionByLanguage on the full record set before computing aggregates.
2. GPA calculation: iterate only the supported slice.
3. Grade distribution counts: only tally supported records.
4. All average calculations (risk score, etc.): only from supported records.
5. Set Card.UnsupportedCount = len(unsupported).
6. Set Card.UnsupportedLanguages = deduplicated sorted list of language strings from unsupported records.
7. Set Card.AnalyzableUnits = len(supported); keep existing TotalUnits (or rename) as the supported-only denominator.
8. Run go test ./... -count=1 — existing tests may need minor fixture updates if they relied on aggregate counts that now exclude unsupported languages. Fix any failures.
deliverables.objective: Modify GenerateCard to call partitionByLanguage and compute all aggregates (GPA, grade distribution, averages) from supported-only records, setting UnsupportedCount, UnsupportedLanguages, and AnalyzableUnits on the returned Card.

nonGoals: Do not modify GenerateCard logic
Do not change Card struct fields
Do not modify badge.go, health.go, detailed.go, full.go, report_tree.go
Do not add new test cases
deliverables.planText:
1. In FormatCardText: if Card.UnsupportedCount > 0, emit a banner line: '⚠️ N units in unsupported languages (lang1, lang2, ...) — not analyzed. Scores for these units are withheld.'
2. In FormatCardText: add denominator clarification in the aggregate section: 'Based on X analyzable units; Y units skipped.'
3. In FormatCardMarkdown: same banner as markdown blockquote or bold text.
4. In FormatCardMarkdown: same denominator clarification.
5. Ensure banner is omitted when UnsupportedCount == 0.
6. Run go test ./... -count=1.
deliverables.objective: Add the unsupported-units banner and denominator clarification to FormatCardText and FormatCardMarkdown in card.go.

nonGoals: Do not modify card.go
Do not add new test cases or testdata
Do not change the analysis registry
deliverables.planText:
1. In internal/report/health.go: Health() must partition records and compute health metrics only from supported units. Unsupported units get N/A grade and do not affect health score.
2. In internal/report/badge.go: badge grade/score reads from supported-only aggregates (should auto-fix once Card is correct, but verify).
3. In internal/report/detailed.go: each unsupported unit's detailed output shows Grade: N/A and Language: <lang> (unsupported).
4. In internal/report/full.go: full report excludes unsupported units from aggregate summaries.
5. In internal/report/report_tree.go: tree view marks unsupported units with N/A status.
6. Run go test ./... -count=1.
deliverables.objective: Update health.go, badge.go, detailed.go, full.go, and report_tree.go to exclude unsupported units from all aggregates and surface N/A grades for unsupported units.

nonGoals: Do not modify production code in card.go, badge.go, health.go, etc.
Do not change the analysis registry
Do not add new analyzers
deliverables.planText:
1. Create testdata/unsupported/hello.swift (or similar) — a trivial file in an unsupported language (Swift).
2. In internal/report/card_test.go: add TestGenerateCard_MixedSupportedAndUnsupported — verify banner present, aggregates exclude unsupported, UnsupportedCount > 0, UnsupportedLanguages non-empty.
3. Add TestGenerateCard_AllSupported — verify no banner, UnsupportedCount == 0.
4. Add TestGenerateCard_AllUnsupported — verify banner present, AnalyzableUnits == 0, GPA is 0 or N/A, UnsupportedCount == total.
5. In internal/report/badge_test.go: add corresponding badge tests verifying badge grade/score ignores unsupported units.
6. Run go test ./... -count=1 and fix any failures.
deliverables.objective: Add testdata fixtures with an unsupported-language file and write test cases for mixed, all-supported, and all-unsupported scenarios in card_test.go and badge_test.go.

nonGoals: Do not introduce new features beyond the banner and aggregate filtering
Do not modify the analysis registry or add new analyzers
Do not change the policy engine
deliverables.planText:
1. Run go test ./... -count=1 — all tests pass.
2. Run go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ — clean build, no warnings.
3. Run go vet ./... — no issues.
4. Manually inspect or run certify against testdata containing unsupported-language fixtures and confirm the banner appears in both text and markdown output.
5. Confirm supported-only aggregates are correct (GPA, distribution, averages).
6. Fix any remaining issues found during integration.
deliverables.objective: Final integration validation: run full test suite, clean build, and verify the report card renders correctly end-to-end with the new banner.
